9	from vllm import envs
10	from vllm.logger import init_logger
11	from vllm.platforms import current_platform
12	
13	logger = init_logger(__name__)
14	
15	try:
16	    import flashinfer.sampling
17	    is_flashinfer_available = True
18	except ImportError:
19	    is_flashinfer_available = False
20	
21	
22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
151	
152	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_k_cutoff
153	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
154	
155	    if p is not None:
156	        cumprob = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
157	        top_p_mask = cumprob <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
158	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # at least one
159	
160	        top_p_count = top_p_mask.sum(dim=-1).unsqueeze(1)
161	        top_p_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_p_count)
162	        elements_to_discard = probs < top_p_cutoff
163	        logits.masked_fill_(elements_to_discard, -float("inf"))
164	
165	    return logits
166	
167	
168	def apply_top_k_top_p(
169	    logits: torch.Tensor,
170	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
171	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
172	) -> torch.Tensor:
173	    """Apply top-k and top-p masks to the logits.
174	
175	    If a top-p is used, this function will sort the logits tensor,
176	    which can be slow for large batches.
177	
178	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
179	    """
180	    if p is None:
181	        if k is None:
182	            return logits
183	
184	        # Avoid sorting vocab for top-k only case.
185	        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)
186	
187	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
188	
189	    if k is not None:
190	        # Apply top-k.
191	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: B
192	        # Get all the top_k values.
193	        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
194	        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
195	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
196	
197	    if p is not None:
198	        # Apply top-p.
199	        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
200	        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
201	        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
202	        # at least one
203	        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
204	        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
205	
206	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
207	    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
208	    return logits
209	
210	
211	def apply_top_k_only(
212	    logits: torch.Tensor,
213	    k: torch.Tensor,
214	) -> torch.Tensor:
215	    """
216	    Apply top-k mask to the logits.
217	
218	    This implementation doesn't involve sorting the entire vocab.
219	
220	    The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
221	    """
222	    no_top_k_mask = k == logits.shape[1]
223	    # Set non-top-k rows to 1 so that we can gather.
224	    k = k.masked_fill(no_top_k_mask, 1)
225	    max_top_k = k.max()
226	    # topk.values tensor has shape [batch_size, max_top_k].
227	    # Convert top k to 0-based index in range [0, max_top_k).
228	    k_index = k.sub_(1).unsqueeze(1)
229	    top_k_mask = logits.topk(max_top_k, dim=1).values.gather(1, k_index.long())
230	    # Handle non-topk rows.
231	    top_k_mask.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask.unsqueeze(1), -float("inf"))
232	    logits.masked_fill_(logits < top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
233	    return logits
234	
235	
236	def random_sample(
237	    probs: torch.Tensor,
238	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
239	) -> torch.Tensor:
240	    """Randomly sample from the probabilities.
241	
242	    We use this function instead of torch.multinomial because torch.multinomial
243	    causes CPU-GPU synchronization.
244	    """
245	    q = torch.empty_like(probs)
246	    # NOTE(woosuk): To batch-process the requests without their own seeds,
247	    # which is the common case, we first assume that every request does
248	    # not have its own seed. Then, we overwrite the values for the requests
249	    # that have their own seeds.
250	    if len(generators) != probs.shape[0]:
251	        q.exponential_()
252	    if generators:
253	        # TODO(woosuk): This can be slow because we handle each request
254	        # one by one. Optimize this.
255	        for i, generator in generators.items():
256	            q[i].exponential_(generator=generator)
257	    return probs.div_(q).argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
258	
259	
260	def flashinfer_sample(
261	    logits: torch.Tensor,
262	    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
263	    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
264	    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
265	) -> torch.Tensor:
266	    """Sample from the logits using FlashInfer.
267	
268	    Statistically, this function is equivalent to the `random_sample` function.
269	    However, this function is faster because it avoids sorting the logits tensor
270	    via rejection sampling.
271	
272	    NOTE: The outputs of this function do not necessarily match the outputs of
273	    the `random_sample` function. It only guarantees that the outputs are
274	    statistically equivalent.
275	
276	    NOTE: This function includes CPU-GPU synchronization, while `random_sample`
277	    does not. Call this function at the end of the forward pass to minimize
278	    the synchronization overhead.
279	    """
280	    assert not (k is None and p is None)
281	    if k is None:
282	        # Top-p only.
283	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
284	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
285	            probs, p, deterministic=True)
286	    elif p is None:
287	        # Top-k only.
288	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
289	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
290	            probs, k, deterministic=True)
291	    else:
292	        # Both top-k and top-p.
293	        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
294	            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
295	
296	    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
297

3	"""A layer that samples the next tokens from the model's outputs."""

5	import torch
6	import torch.nn as nn
7	
8	from vllm.utils import is_pin_memory_available
9	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput
10	from vllm.v1.sample.metadata import SamplingMetadata
11	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.bad_words import apply_bad_words
12	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.penalties import apply_all_penalties
13	from vllm.v1.sample.ops.topk_topp_sampler import TopKTopPSampler

15	_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5

17	
18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
39	
40	        # Use float32 for the logits.
41	        logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
42	        # Apply allowed token ids.
43	        logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
44	        # Apply bad words exclusion.
45	        logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
46	
47	        # Apply logits processors which can impact greedy sampling
48	        for processor in (sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant):
49	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
50	
51	        # Apply penalties (e.g., min_tokens, freq_penalties).
52	        logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
53	        # Sample the next token.
54	        sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
55	        # Convert sampled token ids to int64 (long) type to ensure compatibility
56	        # with subsequent operations that may use these values as indices.
57	        # This conversion is necessary because FlashInfer sampling operations
58	        # return int32 (while PyTorch argmax and topk return int64).
59	        sampled = sampled.long()
60	
61	        # Gather the logprobs of the topk and sampled token (if requested).
62	        # Get logprobs and rank tensors (if requested)
63	        logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
64	            self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
65	
66	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
67	        sampled = sampled.to(torch.int32)
68	
69	        # These are GPU tensors.
70	        sampler_output = SamplerOutput(
71	            # The sampled tokens are expanded to 2D tensor with shape
72	            # [num_requests, 1], where each row represents one generated
73	            # token per request.
74	            sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
75	            logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors,
76	        )
77	        return sampler_output
78	
79	    def apply_temperature(
80	        self,
81	        logits: torch.Tensor,
82	        temp: torch.Tensor,
83	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
84	        # Use in-place division to avoid creating a new tensor.
85	        return logits.div_(temp.unsqueeze(dim=1))
86	
87	    def greedy_sample(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
88	        return logits.argmax(dim=-1).view(-1)
89	
90	    def sample(
91	        self,
92	        logits: torch.Tensor,
93	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
94	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
95	        """Sample logits based on sampling metadata.
96	
97	        The various logits processing functions called in this method
98	        may update the logits tensor in-place.
99	        """
100	
101	        assert not (sampling_metadata.all_greedy
102	                    and sampling_metadata.all_random)
103	        if sampling_metadata.all_random:
104	            greedy_sampled = None
105	        else:
106	            greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits)
107	            if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
108	                return greedy_sampled
109	
110	        assert sampling_metadata.temperature is not None
111	
112	        # Apply temperature.
113	        logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
114	
115	        # Apply logits processors that only apply to random sampling
116	        # (argmax invariant)
117	        for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
118	            logits = processor.apply(logits)
119	
120	        # Apply top_k and/or top_p.
121	        random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
122	            logits,
123	            sampling_metadata.generators,
124	            sampling_metadata.top_k,
125	            sampling_metadata.top_p,
126	        )
127	
128	        if greedy_sampled is None:
129	            return random_sampled
130	
131	        sampled = torch.where(
132	            sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
133	            greedy_sampled,
134	            random_sampled,
135	            out=greedy_sampled,  # Reuse tensor
136	        )
137	        return sampled

139	    def compute_logprobs(self, logits: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
140	        return logits.log_softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
141	
142	    def gather_logprobs(
143	        self,
144	        logprobs: torch.Tensor,
145	        num_logprobs: int,
146	        token_ids: torch.Tensor,
147	    ) -> LogprobsTensors:
148	        """
149	        Gather logprobs for topk and sampled/prompt token.
150	
151	        Args:
152	          logprobs: (num tokens) x (vocab) tensor
153	          num_logprobs: minimum number of logprobs to
154	                        retain per token
155	          token_ids: prompt tokens (if prompt logprobs)
156	                     or sampled tokens (if sampled
157	                     logprobs); 1D token ID tensor
158	                     with (num tokens) elements
159	                     Must be int64.
160	
161	        Returns:
162	          Top-k int indices tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
163	          Top-k float logprobs tensor, (num tokens) x (num_logprobs + 1)
164	          Sampled token rank tensor, (num tokens)
165	        """
166	        assert token_ids.dtype == torch.int64
167	        # Find the topK values.
168	        topk_logprobs, topk_indices = torch.topk(logprobs,
169	                                                 num_logprobs,
170	                                                 dim=-1)
171	
172	        # Get with the logprob of the prompt or sampled token.
173	        token_ids = token_ids.unsqueeze(-1)
174	        token_logprobs = logprobs.gather(-1, token_ids)
175	
176	        # Compute the ranks of the actual token.
177	        token_ranks = (logprobs >= token_logprobs).sum(-1)
178	
179	        # Concatenate together with the topk.
180	        indices = torch.cat((token_ids, topk_indices), dim=1)
181	        logprobs = torch.cat((token_logprobs, topk_logprobs), dim=1)
182	
183	        # Use int32 to reduce the tensor size.
184	        indices = indices.to(torch.int32)
185	
186	        return LogprobsTensors(indices, logprobs, token_ranks)
187

192	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
193	        if not sampling_metadata.no_penalties:

196	                logits,
197	                sampling_metadata.prompt_token_ids,
198	                sampling_metadata.presence_penalties,

22	class TopKTopPSampler(nn.Module):
23	    """
24	    Module that performs optional top-k and top-p filtering followed by
25	    weighted random sampling of logits.
26	
27	    Implementations may update the logits tensor in-place.
28	    """
29	
30	    def __init__(self):
31	        super().__init__()
32	        if current_platform.is_cuda():
33	            if is_flashinfer_available:
34	                flashinfer_version = flashinfer.__version__
35	                if flashinfer_version < "0.2.3":
36	                    logger.warning(
37	                        "FlashInfer version >= 0.2.3 required. "
38	                        "Falling back to default sampling implementation.")
39	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
40	                elif envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False:
41	                    # NOTE(woosuk): The V0 sampler doesn't use FlashInfer for
42	                    # sampling unless VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1 (i.e., by
43	                    # default it is unused). For backward compatibility, we set
44	                    # `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER` as None by default and
45	                    # interpret it differently in V0 and V1 samplers: In V0,
46	                    # None means False, while in V1, None means True. This is
47	                    # why we use the condition
48	                    # `envs.VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False` here.
49	                    logger.info("Using FlashInfer for top-p & top-k sampling.")
50	                    self.forward = self.forward_cuda
51	                else:
52	                    logger.warning(
53	                        "FlashInfer is available, but it is not enabled. "
54	                        "Falling back to the PyTorch-native implementation of "
55	                        "top-p & top-k sampling. For the best performance, "
56	                        "please set VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=1.")
57	                    self.forward = self.forward_native
58	            else:
59	                logger.warning(
60	                    "FlashInfer is not available. Falling back to the PyTorch-"
61	                    "native implementation of top-p & top-k sampling. For the "
62	                    "best performance, please install FlashInfer.")
63	                self.forward = self.forward_native
64	        elif current_platform.is_tpu():
65	            self.forward = self.forward_tpu
66	        else:
67	            self.forward = self.forward_native
68	
69	    def forward_native(
70	        self,
71	        logits: torch.Tensor,
72	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
73	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
74	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
75	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
76	        """
77	        PyTorch-native implementation of top-k and top-p sampling.
78	
79	        The logits tensor may be updated in-place.
80	        """
81	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)
82	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
83	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
84	
85	    def forward_cuda(
86	        self,
87	        logits: torch.Tensor,
88	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
89	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
90	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
91	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
92	        """More optimized implementation for top-k and top-p sampling."""
93	        if k is None and p is None:
94	            # We prefer `random_sample` over `flashinfer_sample` when sorting is
95	            # not needed. This is because `random_sample` does not require
96	            # CPU-GPU synchronization while `flashinfer_sample` does.
97	            probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
98	            return random_sample(probs, generators)
99	        if generators:
100	            logger.warning("FlashInfer 0.2.3+ does not support "
101	                           "per-request generators. Falling back to "
102	                           "PyTorch-native implementation.")
103	            return self.forward_native(logits, generators, k, p)
104	        # flashinfer sampling functions expect contiguous logits.
105	        # In flex_attn/triton_attn fp32 inference, logits can be non-contiguous
106	        # because of slicing operation in logits_processor.
107	        return flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)
108	
109	    def forward_tpu(
110	        self,
111	        logits: torch.Tensor,
112	        generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
113	        k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
114	        p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
115	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
116	        logits = apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)
117	        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
118	        return random_sample(probs, generators)
119	
120	
121	def apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(
122	    logits: torch.Tensor,
123	    k: torch.Tensor,
124	    p: torch.Tensor,
125	) -> torch.Tensor:
126	    """
127	    Apply top-k and top-p optimized for TPU.
128	
129	    This algorithm avoids using torch.scatter which is extremely slow on TPU.
130	    This is achieved by finding a "cut-off" element in the original logit, and
131	    after thresholding the logit using this cut-off, the remaining elements
132	    shall constitute the top-p set.
133	
134	    Note: in the case of tie (i.e. multipple cut-off elements present in the
135	    logit), all tie elements are included in the top-p set. In other words,
136	    this function does not break ties. Instead, these tie tokens have equal
137	    chance of being chosen during final sampling, so we can consider the tie
138	    being broken then.
139	    """
140	    probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1)
141	    probs_sort, _ = probs.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
142	
143	    if k is not None:
144	        top_k_count = probs_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)  # shape: (batch, )
145	        top_k_count = top_k_count.unsqueeze(dim=1)
146	        top_k_cutoff = probs_sort.gather(-1, top_k_count)
147	
148	        # Make sure the no top-k rows are no-op.
149	        no_top_k_mask = (k == logits.shape[1]).unsqueeze(dim=1)
150	        top_k_cutoff.masked_fill_(no_top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

4

9	from vllm.v1.outputs import LogprobsTensors, SamplerOutput

18	class Sampler(nn.Module):
19	
20	    def __init__(self):
21	        super().__init__()
22	        self.topk_topp_sampler = TopKTopPSampler()
23	        self.pin_memory = is_pin_memory_available()
24	
25	    def forward(
26	        self,
27	        logits: torch.Tensor,
28	        sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
29	    ) -> SamplerOutput:
30	        # NOTE(woosuk): Use the original logits (before any penalties or
31	        # temperature scaling) for the top-k logprobs.
32	        # This is different from the V0 sampler, which uses the logits that
33	        # is used for sampling (after penalties and temperature scaling).
34	        # TODO(rob): provide option for logprobs post sampling.
35	        # See https://vllm-dev.slack.com/archives/C07UUL8E61Z/p1735907856007919 # noqa: E501
36	        num_logprobs = sampling_metadata.max_num_logprobs
37	        if num_logprobs is not None:
38	            raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)

## Summary of vLLM Sampler Implementation

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase and here are my comprehensive findings:

### **1. Main Sampler Files**

**File locations:**
- **V0 Sampler (production):** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331952541-xp4hsz/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 1-1000+)
- **V1 Sampler (new):** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331952541-xp4hsz/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`
- **Top-K/Top-P Ops:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331952541-xp4hsz/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

---

### **2. Data Flow: SamplingParams → Intermediate Structures → Sampler**

**Entry point:** `SamplingParams` (defined in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331952541-xp4hsz/vllm/sampling_params.py`)

class SamplingParams:
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    # ... other fields
    
    @cached_property
    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            return SamplingType.GREEDY
        if self.seed is not None:
            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
        return SamplingType.RANDOM
```

**Intermediate structure 1:** `SequenceGroupToSample` (in `sampling_metadata.py`, lines 20-62)

**Intermediate structure 2:** `SamplingMetadata` (in `sampling_metadata.py`, lines 97-188)

        self,

```

**Intermediate structure 3:** `SamplingTensors` (in `sampling_metadata.py`, lines 372-598)

@dataclass

```

---

### **3. Top-P and Top-K Filtering Logic**

#### **V0 Sampler Implementation** (`_apply_top_k_top_p` in `sampler.py`, lines 398-424):

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Step 1: Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Step 2: Apply top-k filtering
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get the k-th largest value for each sequence
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    # Mask all values below the k-th value
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Step 3: Apply top-p filtering
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    # Keep tokens where cumulative sum is > 1-p
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Ensure at least one token is kept
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Step 4: Re-scatter sorted logits back to original vocab positions
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

#### **V1 Sampler Implementation** (`apply_top_k_top_p` in `topk_topp_sampler.py`, lines 168-208):

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)

    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort back
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

#### **Min-P Filtering** (`_apply_min_p` in `sampler.py`, lines 427-441):

```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### **4. Main Sampler Forward Pass (V0)**

**Signature** (`Sampler.forward` in `sampler.py`, lines 225-334):

```python
def forward(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
    """
    Args:
        logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size)
        sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling
    """
    # 1. Initialize sampling tensors from metadata
    self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # 2. Apply min_tokens penalty
    logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # 3. Apply presence/frequency/repetition penalties
    if do_penalties:
        logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
    
    # 4. Apply temperature scaling (in-place division)
    logits = logits.to(torch.float)
    logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    
    # 5. Apply top-k and top-p filtering
    if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                    sampling_tensors.top_ks)
    
    # 6. Apply min_p filtering
    if do_min_p:
        logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
    
    # 7. Compute probabilities (softmax)
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
    logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
    
    # 8. Sample tokens
    maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
        probs, logprobs, sampling_metadata, sampling_tensors,
        include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
        modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
    )
    
    # 9. Build output
    return _build_sampler_output(...)
```

---

### **5. V1 Sampler Forward Pass**

**Signature** (`Sampler.forward` in `v1/sample/sampler.py`, lines 25-77):

```python
def forward(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> SamplerOutput:
    # Compute logprobs from raw logits (before modifications)
    if num_logprobs is not None:
        raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
    
    # Convert to float32
    logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
    
    # Apply allowed token ids mask
    logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # Apply bad words exclusion
    logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # Apply non-argmax-invariant logits processors
    for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant:
        logits = processor.apply(logits)
    
    # Apply penalties
    logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # Sample the next token
    sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # Gather logprobs
    logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
        self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
    
    return SamplerOutput(sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
                        logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors)
```

**V1 Sampling method** (lines 90-137):

```python
def sample(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Greedy sampling path
    if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
        return self.greedy_sample(logits)
    
    # Random sampling path
    greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits) if not sampling_metadata.all_random else None
    
    # Apply temperature
    logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
    
    # Apply argmax-invariant logits processors
    for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
        logits = processor.apply(logits)
    
    # Apply top-k and top-p using TopKTopPSampler
    random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
        logits,
        sampling_metadata.generators,
        sampling_metadata.top_k,
        sampling_metadata.top_p,
    )
    
    # Combine greedy and random results
    if greedy_sampled is None:
        return random_sampled
    
    sampled = torch.where(
        sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
        greedy_sampled,
        random_sampled,
        out=greedy_sampled,
    )
    return sampled
```

---

### **6. Key Function Signatures**

**Sampling Tensor Initialization:**
```python
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    vocab_size: int,
    device: torch.device,
    dtype: torch.dtype,
) -> tuple[SamplingTensors, bool, bool, bool]
```

**Top-K/Top-P Application:**
```python
# V0
_apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,  # shape: (batch_size,)
    k: torch.Tensor,  # shape: (batch_size,)
) -> torch.Tensor

# V1
apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor
```

**Tensor Shapes:**
- Input logits: `(num_tokens, vocab_size)` or `(batch_size, vocab_size)`
- `top_ps`: `(num_tokens,)` or `(batch_size,)`
- `top_ks`: `(num_tokens,)` or `(batch_size,)`
- Masks applied: per-row masking (each sequence gets its own top-k/top-p thresholds)

---

### **7. Key Constants and Thresholds**

```python
_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5  # Threshold for zero temperature detection
_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2      # Minimum temperature threshold
```

**Top-K constraints** (from `sampling_metadata.py`, lines 418-420):
```python
# k should not be greater than the vocab size
top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
```

---

### **8. FlashInfer Integration**

When available, vLLM can use FlashInfer for faster top-k/top-p sampling:
```python
from flashinfer.sampling import top_k_top_p_sampling_from_probs as flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling
```

**V1 FlashInfer paths** (in `topk_topp_sampler.py`, lines 260-296):
```python
def flashinfer_sample(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if k is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, p, deterministic=True)
    elif p is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, k, deterministic=True)
    else:
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
```

---

This comprehensive overview covers the entire sampling pipeline from `SamplingParams` through the actual tensor operations for top-k/top-p filtering.

## Summary of vLLM Sampler Implementation

I've thoroughly explored the vLLM codebase and here are my comprehensive findings:

### **1. Main Sampler Files**

**File locations:**
- **V0 Sampler (production):** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331952541-xp4hsz/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` (lines 1-1000+)
- **V1 Sampler (new):** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331952541-xp4hsz/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`
- **Top-K/Top-P Ops:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331952541-xp4hsz/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

---

### **2. Data Flow: SamplingParams → Intermediate Structures → Sampler**

**Entry point:** `SamplingParams` (defined in `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775331952541-xp4hsz/vllm/sampling_params.py`)

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingParams:
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = 0
    min_p: float = 0.0
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    repetition_penalty: float = 1.0
    seed: Optional[int] = None
    # ... other fields
    
    @cached_property
    def sampling_type(self) -> SamplingType:
        if self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            return SamplingType.GREEDY
        if self.seed is not None:
            return SamplingType.RANDOM_SEED
        return SamplingType.RANDOM
```

**Intermediate structure 1:** `SequenceGroupToSample` (in `sampling_metadata.py`, lines 20-62)

```python
@dataclass
class SequenceGroupToSample:
    seq_ids: list[int]
    sampling_params: SamplingParams
    seq_data: dict[int, SequenceData]
    seq_len: Optional[int]
    query_len: Optional[int]
    generator: Optional[torch.Generator]
    is_prompt: bool
    prompt_logprob_indices: list[int]
    sample_indices: list[int]

    def do_sample(self):
        return len(self.sample_indices) > 0
```

**Intermediate structure 2:** `SamplingMetadata` (in `sampling_metadata.py`, lines 97-188)

```python
class SamplingMetadata:
    def __init__(
        self,
        seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
        selected_token_indices: torch.Tensor,
        categorized_sample_indices: dict[SamplingType, torch.Tensor],
        num_prompts: int,
        skip_sampler_cpu_output: bool = False,
        reuse_sampling_tensors: bool = False,
    ) -> None:
        self.seq_groups = seq_groups
        self.selected_token_indices = selected_token_indices
        self.categorized_sample_indices = categorized_sample_indices
        self.num_prompts = num_prompts
```

**Intermediate structure 3:** `SamplingTensors` (in `sampling_metadata.py`, lines 372-598)

```python
@dataclass
class SamplingTensors:
    temperatures: torch.Tensor
    top_ps: torch.Tensor
    top_ks: torch.Tensor
    min_ps: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor

    output_tokens: torch.Tensor

    @classmethod
    def from_sampling_metadata(
        cls,
        sampling_metadata: "SamplingMetadata",
        vocab_size: int,
        device: torch.device,
        dtype: torch.dtype,
    ) -> tuple["SamplingTensors", bool, bool, bool]:
        # Extracts top_k, top_p, temperatures, etc. from seq_groups
        # Returns (sampling_tensors, do_penalties, do_top_p_top_k, do_min_p)
```

---

### **3. Top-P and Top-K Filtering Logic**

#### **V0 Sampler Implementation** (`_apply_top_k_top_p` in `sampler.py`, lines 398-424):

```python
def _apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,
    k: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Step 1: Sort logits in ascending order
    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    # Step 2: Apply top-k filtering
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
    # Get the k-th largest value for each sequence
    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    # Mask all values below the k-th value
    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Step 3: Apply top-p filtering
    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
    # Keep tokens where cumulative sum is > 1-p
    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False  # Ensure at least one token is kept
    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Step 4: Re-scatter sorted logits back to original vocab positions
    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
                                                    index=logits_idx,
                                                    src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

#### **V1 Sampler Implementation** (`apply_top_k_top_p` in `topk_topp_sampler.py`, lines 168-208):

```python
def apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if p is None:
        if k is None:
            return logits
        return apply_top_k_only(logits, k)

    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)

    if k is not None:
        # Apply top-k
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
        top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
        top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))

    if p is not None:
        # Apply top-p
        probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
        probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1, out=probs_sort)
        top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
        top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
        logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))

    # Re-sort back
    logits = logits_sort.scatter(dim=-1, index=logits_idx, src=logits_sort)
    return logits
```

#### **Min-P Filtering** (`_apply_min_p` in `sampler.py`, lines 427-441):

```python
def _apply_min_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    min_p: torch.Tensor,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
    return logits
```

---

### **4. Main Sampler Forward Pass (V0)**

**Signature** (`Sampler.forward` in `sampler.py`, lines 225-334):

```python
def forward(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> Optional[SamplerOutput]:
    """
    Args:
        logits: (num_tokens, vocab_size)
        sampling_metadata: Metadata for sampling
    """
    # 1. Initialize sampling tensors from metadata
    self._init_sampling_tensors(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # 2. Apply min_tokens penalty
    logits = _apply_min_tokens_penalty(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # 3. Apply presence/frequency/repetition penalties
    if do_penalties:
        logits = apply_penalties(logits, sampling_tensors.prompt_tokens,
                                sampling_tensors.output_tokens,
                                sampling_tensors.presence_penalties,
                                sampling_tensors.frequency_penalties,
                                sampling_tensors.repetition_penalties)
    
    # 4. Apply temperature scaling (in-place division)
    logits = logits.to(torch.float)
    logits.div_(sampling_tensors.temperatures.unsqueeze(dim=1))
    
    # 5. Apply top-k and top-p filtering
    if do_top_p_top_k and flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling is None:
        logits = _apply_top_k_top_p(logits, sampling_tensors.top_ps,
                                    sampling_tensors.top_ks)
    
    # 6. Apply min_p filtering
    if do_min_p:
        logits = _apply_min_p(logits, sampling_tensors.min_ps)
    
    # 7. Compute probabilities (softmax)
    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
    logprobs = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1, dtype=torch.float)
    
    # 8. Sample tokens
    maybe_deferred_sample_results, maybe_sampled_tokens_tensor = _sample(
        probs, logprobs, sampling_metadata, sampling_tensors,
        include_gpu_probs_tensor=self.include_gpu_probs_tensor,
        modify_greedy_probs=self._should_modify_greedy_probs_inplace,
    )
    
    # 9. Build output
    return _build_sampler_output(...)
```

---

### **5. V1 Sampler Forward Pass**

**Signature** (`Sampler.forward` in `v1/sample/sampler.py`, lines 25-77):

```python
def forward(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> SamplerOutput:
    # Compute logprobs from raw logits (before modifications)
    if num_logprobs is not None:
        raw_logprobs = self.compute_logprobs(logits)
    
    # Convert to float32
    logits = logits.to(torch.float32)
    
    # Apply allowed token ids mask
    logits = self.apply_allowed_token_ids(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # Apply bad words exclusion
    logits = self.apply_bad_words(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # Apply non-argmax-invariant logits processors
    for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.non_argmax_invariant:
        logits = processor.apply(logits)
    
    # Apply penalties
    logits = self.apply_penalties(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # Sample the next token
    sampled = self.sample(logits, sampling_metadata)
    
    # Gather logprobs
    logprobs_tensors = None if num_logprobs is None else \
        self.gather_logprobs(raw_logprobs, num_logprobs, token_ids=sampled)
    
    return SamplerOutput(sampled_token_ids=sampled.unsqueeze(-1),
                        logprobs_tensors=logprobs_tensors)
```

**V1 Sampling method** (lines 90-137):

```python
def sample(
    self,
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    # Greedy sampling path
    if sampling_metadata.all_greedy:
        return self.greedy_sample(logits)
    
    # Random sampling path
    greedy_sampled = self.greedy_sample(logits) if not sampling_metadata.all_random else None
    
    # Apply temperature
    logits = self.apply_temperature(logits, sampling_metadata.temperature)
    
    # Apply argmax-invariant logits processors
    for processor in sampling_metadata.logitsprocs.argmax_invariant:
        logits = processor.apply(logits)
    
    # Apply top-k and top-p using TopKTopPSampler
    random_sampled = self.topk_topp_sampler(
        logits,
        sampling_metadata.generators,
        sampling_metadata.top_k,
        sampling_metadata.top_p,
    )
    
    # Combine greedy and random results
    if greedy_sampled is None:
        return random_sampled
    
    sampled = torch.where(
        sampling_metadata.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS,
        greedy_sampled,
        random_sampled,
        out=greedy_sampled,
    )
    return sampled
```

---

### **6. Key Function Signatures**

**Sampling Tensor Initialization:**
```python
SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata(
    sampling_metadata: SamplingMetadata,
    vocab_size: int,
    device: torch.device,
    dtype: torch.dtype,
) -> tuple[SamplingTensors, bool, bool, bool]
```

**Top-K/Top-P Application:**
```python
# V0
_apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    p: torch.Tensor,  # shape: (batch_size,)
    k: torch.Tensor,  # shape: (batch_size,)
) -> torch.Tensor

# V1
apply_top_k_top_p(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
) -> torch.Tensor
```

**Tensor Shapes:**
- Input logits: `(num_tokens, vocab_size)` or `(batch_size, vocab_size)`
- `top_ps`: `(num_tokens,)` or `(batch_size,)`
- `top_ks`: `(num_tokens,)` or `(batch_size,)`
- Masks applied: per-row masking (each sequence gets its own top-k/top-p thresholds)

---

### **7. Key Constants and Thresholds**

```python
_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-5  # Threshold for zero temperature detection
_MAX_TEMP = 1e-2      # Minimum temperature threshold
```

**Top-K constraints** (from `sampling_metadata.py`, lines 418-420):
```python
# k should not be greater than the vocab size
top_k = min(sampling_params.top_k, vocab_size)
top_k = vocab_size if top_k < 1 else top_k
```

---

### **8. FlashInfer Integration**

When available, vLLM can use FlashInfer for faster top-k/top-p sampling:
```python
from flashinfer.sampling import top_k_top_p_sampling_from_probs as flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling
```

**V1 FlashInfer paths** (in `topk_topp_sampler.py`, lines 260-296):
```python
def flashinfer_sample(
    logits: torch.Tensor,
    k: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    p: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if k is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_p_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, p, deterministic=True)
    elif p is None:
        probs = logits.softmax(dim=-1, dtype=torch.float32)
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_sampling_from_probs(
            probs, k, deterministic=True)
    else:
        next_token_ids = flashinfer.sampling.top_k_top_p_sampling_from_logits(
            logits, k, p, deterministic=True)
    return next_token_ids.view(-1)
```

input.command:
cat > /tmp/vllm_top_p_top_k_exploration.txt << 'EOF'
# vLLM top_p and top_k Parameter Flow Analysis

## 1. CORE DATA STRUCTURES

### 1.1 SamplingParams (vllm/sampling_params.py)
- **Location**: Main parameter definition class
- **Fields**:
  - `top_p: float = 1.0` (line 212)
  - `top_k: int = 0` (line 213)
- **Validation** (lines 417-425):
  - top_p must be in (0, 1]
  - top_k must be >= 0 or -1 (treated as disabled)
  - top_k must be an integer
- **Special handling** (lines 377-382):
  - When temperature < epsilon: top_p set to 1.0, top_k set to 0 (greedy sampling)
- **Factory method**: `from_optional()` (lines 252-325) handles default values

### 1.2 SamplingMetadata (vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py)
- **Container for metadata during sampling**
- **Data flow**: SequenceGroupToSample contains sampling_params
- **SequenceGroupToSample fields** (lines 19-50):
  - sampling_params: SamplingParams (holds top_p, top_k)
  - seq_ids, seq_data, seq_len, query_len, generator, etc.

### 1.3 SamplingTensors (vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py lines 372-597)
- **Purpose**: Converts SamplingParams into GPU tensors
- **Tensor fields** (lines 376-384):
  - `temperatures: torch.Tensor`
  - `top_ps: torch.Tensor` (extracted from sampling_params.top_p)
  - `top_ks: torch.Tensor` (extracted from sampling_params.top_k)
  - `min_ps, presence_penalties, frequency_penalties, repetition_penalties`
  - `prompt_tokens, output_tokens`
- **Creation** (lines 386-495):
  - `from_sampling_metadata()` extracts top_p/top_k from each SamplingParams
  - Creates lists then converts to pinned tensors for GPU transfer
- **Processing** (lines 415-420):
  - top_k clamped to vocab_size
  - Tracks which sequences use top_p (top_p < 1.0) and top_k (0 < top_k < vocab_size)
  - Uses lists to accumulate values, then `from_lists()` to create tensors

---

## 2. LAYER 1: API/PROTOCOL LEVEL

### 2.1 OpenAI Protocol (vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py)
- **ChatCompletionRequest** (line 245):
  - `top_p: Optional[float] = None`
- **CompletionRequest** (line 761):
  - `top_p: Optional[float] = None`
- **Both request types** (lines 261, 766):
  - `top_k: Optional[int] = None`
- **Default values** (lines 429-430, 870-871):
  - top_p: 1.0
  - top_k: 0
- **Conversion to SamplingParams** (lines 468-521):
  - Extracts top_p, top_k from request
  - Falls back to defaults if None
  - Passes to SamplingParams constructor

---

## 3. LAYER 2: LEGACY V0 PIPELINE

### 3.1 Sampler Layer (vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py)
- **Forward pass** (lines 225-334):
  - Line 216: Creates SamplingTensors from SamplingMetadata
  - Line 222: `self._do_top_p_top_k` flag set based on SamplingTensors
  - Line 285-286: Applies top_k/top_p filtering via `_apply_top_k_top_p()`
- **Top-k/Top-p filtering** (lines 398-424):
  - Sorts logits in ascending order
  - Applies top-k mask (line 406: `top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)`)
  - Applies top-p mask based on cumulative probability
  - Re-sorts logits back to original order
- **Flashinfer integration** (line 284):
  - Optional: Uses flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling for GPU optimization
- **Multinomial sampling** (lines 550-562):
  - Can use FlashInfer for top-k/top-p sampling

### 3.2 Worker Model Runner (vllm/worker/model_runner.py)
- **Calculates maximum memory usage**:
  - Line 1331: Sets top_p=0.99, top_k=vocab_size-1 for profiling

---

## 4. LAYER 3: V1 PIPELINE (Modern Implementation)

### 4.1 GPU Input Batch (vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py)
- **Input batch tracks sampling parameters as tensors**
- **Data structures** (lines 122-151):
  - `temperature, temperature_cpu_tensor, temperature_cpu`
  - `top_p, top_p_cpu_tensor, top_p_cpu` (float32)
  - `top_k, top_k_cpu_tensor, top_k_cpu` (int32)
  - Separate CPU and GPU versions with pinned memory
- **Request tracking** (lines 141, 151):
  - `top_p_reqs: set[str]` - tracks which requests have top_p < 1
  - `top_k_reqs: set[str]` - tracks which requests have 0 < top_k < vocab_size
- **Adding requests** (lines 266-344):
  - Line 312: `top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p`
  - Line 315-320: `top_k` extracted, clamped to vocab_size if out of range
  - Line 313-314: Only track top_p_reqs if top_p < 1.0
  - Line 316-317: Only track top_k_reqs if 0 < top_k < vocab_size
- **Removing requests** (lines 406-407):
  - Cleans up from tracking sets
- **Batch updates** (lines 456-459):
  - Swaps values when batch is reordered
- **Preparing for GPU** (lines 607-610):
  - `copy_slice()` transfers CPU tensors to GPU
  - Conditional: only if `not no_top_p` and `not no_top_k`
- **Properties** (lines 734-739):
  - `no_top_p`: True if no requests use top_p (len(top_p_reqs) == 0)
  - `no_top_k`: True if no requests use top_k (len(top_k_reqs) == 0)

### 4.2 V1 Sampling Metadata (vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py)
- **Dataclass** (lines 12-43):
  - `temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]`
  - `top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]` (line 19)
  - `top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]` (line 20)
  - `all_greedy, all_random` flags
  - Generator dict, penalties, logprobs config, etc.

### 4.3 V1 Sampler (vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py)
- **Sampling forward** (lines 25-77):
  - Line 120-126: Calls `topk_topp_sampler` with top_k and top_p
  - Passes to TopKTopPSampler
- **Sample method** (lines 90-137):

### 4.4 TopKTopPSampler (vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py)
- **Flexible implementation** (lines 22-67):
  - Selects backend based on platform (CUDA, TPU, CPU)
  - CUDA: Uses FlashInfer if available and enabled
  - TPU: Custom optimized implementation (avoid slow scatter ops)
  - Default: PyTorch native implementation
- **Native implementation** (lines 69-83):
  - Calls `apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)`
  - Computes softmax and samples randomly
- **CUDA backend** (lines 85-107):
  - Optimized: skips sorting if both k and p are None
  - Falls back to native if generators present (FlashInfer doesn't support per-request generators)
  - Contiguous check for FlashInfer compatibility
- **TPU backend** (lines 109-118):
  - Calls `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)`
  - Optimized to avoid scatter operations
- **apply_top_k_top_p()** (handles k and p filtering)
- **apply_top_k_only()** (handles k-only filtering)

---

## 5. DATA FLOW SUMMARY

```
API Request
  ↓
OpenAI Protocol (top_p, top_k extracted)
  ↓
SamplingParams (validated and stored)
  ↓
Engine/Scheduler
  ↓
[V0 PATH]
  ↓
SamplingMetadata → SamplingTensors
  ↓
Sampler Layer (_apply_top_k_top_p)
  ↓
GPU Execution

[V1 PATH]
  ↓
InputBatch (GPU batch manager)
  ├─ Stores top_p_cpu, top_k_cpu
  ├─ Tracks top_p_reqs, top_k_reqs
  ├─ Transfers to GPU: top_p, top_k tensors
  ↓
GPU Model Runner
  ↓
SamplingMetadata (V1 version)
  ├─ top_p: torch.Tensor
  ├─ top_k: torch.Tensor
  ↓
V1 Sampler
  ↓
TopKTopPSampler
  ├─ FlashInfer (CUDA)
  ├─ TPU-optimized (TPU)
  └─ PyTorch native (CPU)
  ↓
Sampled Token IDs
```

---

## 6. KEY FILES SUMMARY

### Core Infrastructure
- `/vllm/sampling_params.py` - SamplingParams definition
- `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py` - SamplingMetadata, SamplingTensors
- `/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` - API request definitions

### V0 (Legacy)
- `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py` - Main sampling layer
- `/vllm/worker/model_runner.py` - Worker process integration
- `/vllm/engine/llm_engine.py` - Engine integration

### V1 (Modern)

- `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py` - V1 sampler
- `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py` - Top-k/Top-p operations
- `/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_model_runner.py` - V1 GPU model runner

---

## 7. SERIALIZATION & CONVERSION POINTS

1. **SamplingParams → SamplingMetadata**:
   - sampling_params.top_p → extracted in _prepare_seq_groups
   - sampling_params.top_k → extracted in _prepare_seq_groups

2. **SamplingMetadata → SamplingTensors**:
   - Lists of top_p/top_k values created from each seq_group.sampling_params
   - Converted to torch tensors with pinned memory

3. **V1 GPU Batch**:
   - CPU arrays (numpy) for top_p_cpu, top_k_cpu
   - Async transfer to GPU via copy_slice()
   - Optional: only if optimization flags allow

4. **To GPU/Model Executor**:
   - Tensors passed to sampler via SamplingMetadata (V0) or GPU batch (V1)
   - FlashInfer or PyTorch kernel receives tensor of top_p values, tensor of top_k values

---

- `/tests/v1/tpu/test_topk_topp_sampler.py`

---

## 9. IMPORTANT NOTES FOR ADDING NEW PARAMETERS

1. **Add to SamplingParams** (sampling_params.py):

6. **V1 Sampler** (v1/sample/sampler.py, v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py):
   - Pass through to TopKTopPSampler
   - Implement filtering logic in all backends (native, CUDA/FlashInfer, TPU)

---

### **API/PROTOCOL LAYER**

**File:** `/vllm/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`
- `ChatCompletionRequest.top_p` (line 245)
- `ChatCompletionRequest.top_k` (line 261)
- `CompletionRequest.top_p` (line 761)
- `CompletionRequest.top_k` (line 766)

- Extraction & conversion in `to_sampling_params()` methods (lines 468-521, 916-961)

---

### **V0 METADATA & TENSORS (Legacy Path)**

**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/sampling_metadata.py`

**SamplingTensors class (lines 372-597):**
- Line 377: `top_ps: torch.Tensor` - batch of top_p values
- Line 378: `top_ks: torch.Tensor` - batch of top_k values
- `from_sampling_metadata()` (lines 386-495):
  - Extracts `top_p` and `top_k` from each sequence group's `sampling_params`

- Lines 545-550: `top_ps_t` tensor created with device="cpu", pinned memory
- Lines 575-580: `top_ks_t` tensor created as int32

---

### **V0 EXECUTION LAYER**

**File:** `/vllm/model_executor/layers/sampler.py`

**Sampler class (lines 169-349):**
- `forward()` (lines 225-334):
  - Line 216-223: Initializes sampling tensors via `SamplingTensors.from_sampling_metadata()`
  - Sets `self._do_top_p_top_k` flag (line 222)
  - Line 284-286: Conditionally applies top-k/top-p filtering
- `_apply_top_k_top_p()` (lines 398-424):
  - Sorts logits in ascending order
  - Applies top-k mask: `top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)`
  - Applies top-p mask based on cumulative probability threshold
  - Re-sorts back to original order
- Flashinfer integration (line 284): Optional use of `flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling` for GPU acceleration
- Multinomial sampling (lines 550-562): `_top_k_top_p_multinomial_with_flashinfer()`

---

### **V1 BATCH MANAGEMENT (Modern Path)**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`

**InputBatch class - Storage (lines 122-151):**
- Line 133-135: `self.top_p` (torch.Tensor), `self.top_p_cpu_tensor`, `self.top_p_cpu` (numpy)
- Line 136-140: Same for top_p with pinned memory for async H2D transfer
- Line 143-151: Same for `top_k` as int32 tensors
- Line 141: `self.top_p_reqs: set[str]` - optimization: tracks which requests use top_p
- Line 151: `self.top_k_reqs: set[str]` - optimization: tracks which requests use top_k

**Adding requests (lines 266-344):**
- Line 312: `self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p`
- Lines 313-314: Adds to `top_p_reqs` only if `top_p < 1`
- Lines 315-320: Extracts top_k, clamps to vocab_size if >= vocab_size, stores in `top_k_cpu[req_index]`
- Lines 316-317: Adds to `top_k_reqs` only if `0 < top_k < vocab_size`

**Batch operations (lines 406-407, 456-459, 555-556):**
- Removes from tracking sets
- Swaps CPU arrays when batch is reordered
- Fills empty indices with last request's values

**GPU transfer (lines 607-610):**
- `copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)` - only if `not no_top_p`
- `copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)` - only if `not no_top_k`
- Lines 646-647: Passes to SamplingMetadata (None if optimization allows skipping)

**Properties (lines 734-739):**
- `no_top_p`: Returns `len(self.top_p_reqs) == 0` (optimization: no requests need it)
- `no_top_k`: Returns `len(self.top_k_reqs) == 0`

---

### **V1 METADATA**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

**SamplingMetadata dataclass:**
- Line 19: `top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]` - tensor of top_p values for batch
- Line 20: `top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]` - tensor of top_k values for batch
- Line 22: `generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]` - per-request generators

---

### **V1 EXECUTION**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`

**Sampler class (lines 18-197):**
- `sample()` (lines 90-137):
  - Line 112: Applies temperature scaling
  - Lines 121-126: Calls `self.topk_topp_sampler()` with:
    - `sampling_metadata.generators`
    - `sampling_metadata.top_k`
    - `sampling_metadata.top_p`

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

**TopKTopPSampler class (lines 22-118):**
- Constructor (lines 30-67): Selects backend based on platform
  - CUDA: Uses FlashInfer if available and `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False`
  - TPU: Custom TPU-optimized implementation
  - CPU: PyTorch native fallback

- `forward_native()` (lines 69-83):
  - Calls `apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)` for filtering
  - Computes softmax, performs random sampling via `random_sample()`

- `forward_cuda()` (lines 85-107):
  - Optimization: If k and p are None, skips FlashInfer and uses native sampling
  - Falls back to native if generators present (FlashInfer doesn't support per-request generators)
  - Calls `flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)`

- `forward_tpu()` (lines 109-118):
  - Calls `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)` - optimized for TPU
  - Avoids scatter operations which are slow on TPU

- `apply_top_k_top_p()`: Core filtering logic (after line 119)
- `apply_top_k_only()`: Handles k-only filtering
- `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` (lines 121-150+): TPU-specific implementation

---

### **201 FILES CONTAINING top_p/top_k**

**Key categories:**
- **Core infrastructure** (6 files): sampling_params.py, sampling_metadata.py, protocol.py, config.py, etc.
- **V0 workers** (12 files): model_runner.py, worker.py, tpu_worker.py, etc. in `/vllm/worker/`
- **V1 components** (11 files): gpu_model_runner.py, gpu_input_batch.py, sampler.py, metadata.py, etc.
- **Tests** (40+ files): samplers/, v1/sample/, v1/tpu/ test directories
- **Examples** (50+ files): offline_inference/, online_serving/ examples
- **Documentation** (10+ files): markdown docs and README files

---

### **COMPLETE DATA FLOW**

```
1. API Request → OpenAI Protocol (top_p, top_k extracted)
2. → SamplingParams (validated, stored with defaults)
3. → Engine/Scheduler
   ├─ V0: SamplingMetadata
   │   └─ SamplingTensors (lists → pinned GPU tensors)
   │       └─ Sampler Layer._apply_top_k_top_p(logits, top_ps, top_ks)
   │           └─ GPU kernel (FlashInfer or native)
   │
   └─ V1: InputBatch
       ├─ CPU arrays (top_p_cpu, top_k_cpu, numpy)
       ├─ GPU tensors (top_p, top_k, torch)
       ├─ Request tracking sets (top_p_reqs, top_k_reqs)
       └─ GPU Model Runner
           └─ V1 SamplingMetadata (tensor fields)
               └─ V1 Sampler
                   └─ TopKTopPSampler (selects backend)
                       ├─ FlashInfer (CUDA)
                       ├─ apply_top_k_top_p_tpu (TPU)
                       └─ apply_top_k_top_p (PyTorch native)
                           └─ Sampled tokens
```

---

### **SERIALIZATION & CONVERSION POINTS**

1. **SamplingParams → Lists**: Each sequence group's sampling_params.top_p/top_k extracted
2. **Lists → SamplingTensors**: Converted to pinned torch tensors in `from_lists()`
3. **V1 CPU → GPU**: `copy_slice()` transfers numpy arrays to GPU tensors
4. **Conditional optimization**: Sets to None if `no_top_p` or `no_top_k` flags true
5. **To kernels**: Batch tensors passed directly to sampler operators

---

### **CHECKLIST FOR ADDING NEW SAMPLING PARAMETER**

1. **SamplingParams** (sampling_params.py):
   - Add field with default value
   - Add validation in `_verify_args()`
   - Add to `from_optional()` factory
   - Add to `__repr__()`

2. **SamplingTensors** (sampling_metadata.py):
   - Add tensor field
   - Extract in `from_sampling_metadata()` method
   - Create lists and convert in `from_lists()`

3. **OpenAI Protocol** (entrypoints/openai/protocol.py):
   - Add field to request classes
   - Add to `_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS` dict
   - Extract in `to_sampling_params()` methods

4. **V1 GPU Batch** (v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py):
   - Add CPU tensor pair (cpu_tensor, cpu array)
   - Add GPU tensor
   - Add request tracking set (if optimization needed)
   - Store in `add_request()` from sampling_params
   - Handle in swap/remove operations
   - Transfer in `prepare_input_batch()`
   - Add property `no_param` check

5. **V1 SamplingMetadata** (v1/sample/metadata.py):
   - Add tensor field with Optional type

6. **V1 Sampler** (v1/sample/sampler.py):
   - Pass to TopKTopPSampler

7. **TopKTopPSampler** (v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py):
   - Implement in all 3 backends (native, CUDA/FlashInfer, TPU)

  - Sorts logits in ascending order
  - Applies top-k mask: `top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)`
  - Applies top-p mask based on cumulative probability threshold
  - Re-sorts back to original order
- Flashinfer integration (line 284): Optional use of `flashinfer_top_k_top_p_sampling` for GPU acceleration
- Multinomial sampling (lines 550-562): `_top_k_top_p_multinomial_with_flashinfer()`

---

### **V1 BATCH MANAGEMENT (Modern Path)**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py`

**InputBatch class - Storage (lines 122-151):**
- Line 133-135: `self.top_p` (torch.Tensor), `self.top_p_cpu_tensor`, `self.top_p_cpu` (numpy)
- Line 136-140: Same for top_p with pinned memory for async H2D transfer
- Line 143-151: Same for `top_k` as int32 tensors
- Line 141: `self.top_p_reqs: set[str]` - optimization: tracks which requests use top_p
- Line 151: `self.top_k_reqs: set[str]` - optimization: tracks which requests use top_k

**Adding requests (lines 266-344):**
- Line 312: `self.top_p_cpu[req_index] = sampling_params.top_p`
- Lines 313-314: Adds to `top_p_reqs` only if `top_p < 1`
- Lines 315-320: Extracts top_k, clamps to vocab_size if >= vocab_size, stores in `top_k_cpu[req_index]`
- Lines 316-317: Adds to `top_k_reqs` only if `0 < top_k < vocab_size`

**Batch operations (lines 406-407, 456-459, 555-556):**
- Removes from tracking sets
- Swaps CPU arrays when batch is reordered
- Fills empty indices with last request's values

**GPU transfer (lines 607-610):**
- `copy_slice(self.top_p_cpu_tensor, self.top_p, num_reqs)` - only if `not no_top_p`
- `copy_slice(self.top_k_cpu_tensor, self.top_k, num_reqs)` - only if `not no_top_k`
- Lines 646-647: Passes to SamplingMetadata (None if optimization allows skipping)

**Properties (lines 734-739):**
- `no_top_p`: Returns `len(self.top_p_reqs) == 0` (optimization: no requests need it)
- `no_top_k`: Returns `len(self.top_k_reqs) == 0`

---

### **V1 METADATA**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/metadata.py`

**SamplingMetadata dataclass:**
- Line 19: `top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]` - tensor of top_p values for batch
- Line 20: `top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]` - tensor of top_k values for batch
- Line 22: `generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]` - per-request generators

---

### **V1 EXECUTION**

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/sampler.py`

**Sampler class (lines 18-197):**
- `sample()` (lines 90-137):
  - Line 112: Applies temperature scaling
  - Lines 121-126: Calls `self.topk_topp_sampler()` with:
    - `sampling_metadata.generators`
    - `sampling_metadata.top_k`
    - `sampling_metadata.top_p`

**File:** `/vllm/v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py`

**TopKTopPSampler class (lines 22-118):**
- Constructor (lines 30-67): Selects backend based on platform
  - CUDA: Uses FlashInfer if available and `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER is not False`
  - TPU: Custom TPU-optimized implementation
  - CPU: PyTorch native fallback

- `forward_native()` (lines 69-83):
  - Calls `apply_top_k_top_p(logits, k, p)` for filtering
  - Computes softmax, performs random sampling via `random_sample()`

- `forward_cuda()` (lines 85-107):
  - Optimization: If k and p are None, skips FlashInfer and uses native sampling
  - Falls back to native if generators present (FlashInfer doesn't support per-request generators)
  - Calls `flashinfer_sample(logits.contiguous(), k, p, generators)`

- `forward_tpu()` (lines 109-118):
  - Calls `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu(logits, k, p)` - optimized for TPU
  - Avoids scatter operations which are slow on TPU

- `apply_top_k_top_p()`: Core filtering logic (after line 119)
- `apply_top_k_only()`: Handles k-only filtering
- `apply_top_k_top_p_tpu()` (lines 121-150+): TPU-specific implementation

---

### **201 FILES CONTAINING top_p/top_k**

**Key categories:**
- **Core infrastructure** (6 files): sampling_params.py, sampling_metadata.py, protocol.py, config.py, etc.
- **V0 workers** (12 files): model_runner.py, worker.py, tpu_worker.py, etc. in `/vllm/worker/`
- **V1 components** (11 files): gpu_model_runner.py, gpu_input_batch.py, sampler.py, metadata.py, etc.
- **Tests** (40+ files): samplers/, v1/sample/, v1/tpu/ test directories
- **Examples** (50+ files): offline_inference/, online_serving/ examples
- **Documentation** (10+ files): markdown docs and README files

---

### **COMPLETE DATA FLOW**

```
1. API Request → OpenAI Protocol (top_p, top_k extracted)
2. → SamplingParams (validated, stored with defaults)
3. → Engine/Scheduler
   ├─ V0: SamplingMetadata
   │   └─ SamplingTensors (lists → pinned GPU tensors)
   │       └─ Sampler Layer._apply_top_k_top_p(logits, top_ps, top_ks)
   │           └─ GPU kernel (FlashInfer or native)
   │
   └─ V1: InputBatch
       ├─ CPU arrays (top_p_cpu, top_k_cpu, numpy)
       ├─ GPU tensors (top_p, top_k, torch)
       ├─ Request tracking sets (top_p_reqs, top_k_reqs)
       └─ GPU Model Runner
           └─ V1 SamplingMetadata (tensor fields)
               └─ V1 Sampler
                   └─ TopKTopPSampler (selects backend)
                       ├─ FlashInfer (CUDA)
                       ├─ apply_top_k_top_p_tpu (TPU)
                       └─ apply_top_k_top_p (PyTorch native)
                           └─ Sampled tokens
```

---

### **SERIALIZATION & CONVERSION POINTS**

1. **SamplingParams → Lists**: Each sequence group's sampling_params.top_p/top_k extracted
2. **Lists → SamplingTensors**: Converted to pinned torch tensors in `from_lists()`
3. **V1 CPU → GPU**: `copy_slice()` transfers numpy arrays to GPU tensors
4. **Conditional optimization**: Sets to None if `no_top_p` or `no_top_k` flags true
5. **To kernels**: Batch tensors passed directly to sampler operators

---

### **CHECKLIST FOR ADDING NEW SAMPLING PARAMETER**

1. **SamplingParams** (sampling_params.py):
   - Add field with default value
   - Add validation in `_verify_args()`
   - Add to `from_optional()` factory
   - Add to `__repr__()`

2. **SamplingTensors** (sampling_metadata.py):
   - Add tensor field
   - Extract in `from_sampling_metadata()` method
   - Create lists and convert in `from_lists()`

3. **OpenAI Protocol** (entrypoints/openai/protocol.py):
   - Add field to request classes
   - Add to `_DEFAULT_SAMPLING_PARAMS` dict
   - Extract in `to_sampling_params()` methods

4. **V1 GPU Batch** (v1/worker/gpu_input_batch.py):
   - Add CPU tensor pair (cpu_tensor, cpu array)
   - Add GPU tensor
   - Add request tracking set (if optimization needed)
   - Store in `add_request()` from sampling_params
   - Handle in swap/remove operations
   - Transfer in `prepare_input_batch()`
   - Add property `no_param` check

5. **V1 SamplingMetadata** (v1/sample/metadata.py):
   - Add tensor field with Optional type

6. **V1 Sampler** (v1/sample/sampler.py):
   - Pass to TopKTopPSampler

7. **TopKTopPSampler** (v1/sample/ops/topk_topp_sampler.py):
   - Implement in all 3 backends (native, CUDA/FlashInfer, TPU)

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright contributors to the vLLM project

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Optional

import torch

from vllm.v1.sample.logits_processor import LogitsProcessorManager


@dataclass
class SamplingMetadata:

    temperature: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    all_greedy: bool
    all_random: bool

    top_p: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    top_k: Optional[torch.Tensor]

    generators: dict[int, torch.Generator]

    # None means no logprobs, 0 means sampled token logprobs only
    max_num_logprobs: Optional[int]

    no_penalties: bool
    prompt_token_ids: Optional[torch.Tensor]
    frequency_penalties: torch.Tensor
    presence_penalties: torch.Tensor
    repetition_penalties: torch.Tensor

    output_token_ids: list[list[int]]

    # `allowed_token_ids_mask` is a 2D bool tensor of shape (max batch size,
    # vocab size).
    allowed_token_ids_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor]

    # req_index -> bad_words_token_ids
    bad_words_token_ids: dict[int, list[list[int]]]

    # Loaded logits processors
    logitsprocs: LogitsProcessorManager

395	    return logits

398	def _apply_top_k_top_p(
399	    logits: torch.Tensor,
400	    p: torch.Tensor,
401	    k: torch.Tensor,
402	) -> torch.Tensor:
403	    logits_sort, logits_idx = logits.sort(dim=-1, descending=False)
404	
405	    # Apply top-k.
406	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.size(1) - k.to(torch.long)
407	    # Get all the top_k values.
408	    top_k_mask = logits_sort.gather(1, top_k_mask.unsqueeze(dim=1))
409	    top_k_mask = logits_sort < top_k_mask
410	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_k_mask, -float("inf"))
411	
412	    # Apply top-p.
413	    probs_sort = logits_sort.softmax(dim=-1)
414	    probs_sum = probs_sort.cumsum(dim=-1)
415	    top_p_mask = probs_sum <= 1 - p.unsqueeze(dim=1)
416	    # at least one
417	    top_p_mask[:, -1] = False
418	    logits_sort.masked_fill_(top_p_mask, -float("inf"))
419	
420	    # Re-sort the probabilities.
421	    logits = torch.empty_like(logits_sort).scatter_(dim=-1,
422	                                                    index=logits_idx,
423	                                                    src=logits_sort)
424	    return logits

428	    logits: torch.Tensor,

430	) -> torch.Tensor:

432	    Adapted from
433	    https://github.com/oobabooga/text-generation-webui/blob/3146124ec01f02c8fb1650a6517cf1b60b537aaf/modules/sampler_hijack.py#L16C17-L16C17
434	    """
435	    probs = torch.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
436	    top_probs, _ = probs.max(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
437	    scaled_min_p = min_p.unsqueeze_(dim=1) * top_probs
438	    tokens_to_remove = probs < scaled_min_p
439	    logits = logits.masked_fill_(tokens_to_remove, -float("inf"))
440	
441	    return logits

445	    selected_seq_groups: list[SequenceGroupToSample],
446	    samples: torch.Tensor,
447	) -> SampleResultType:
448	    """Run greedy sampling on a given samples.
449	
450	    Args:
451	        selected_seq_groups: A list of sequence groups batched.
452	        samples: (num_selected_samples,) A tensor of samples. The length of
453	            samples could be smaller than selected_seq_groups if

456	        Tuple of (next_token_ids, parent_ids). The length of returned list is
457	        same as the length of selected_seq_groups. If the corresponding
458	        seq_group has do_sample=False, tuple contains ([], [])
459	    """
